root_text.an5.50:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā nārado pāṭaliputte viharati kukkuṭārāme.
root_text.an5.50:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena muṇḍassa rañño bhaddā devī kālaṅkatā hoti piyā manāpā.
root_text.an5.50:1.3: So bhaddāya deviyā kālaṅkatāya piyāya manāpāya neva nhāyati na vilimpati na bhattaṁ bhuñjati na kammantaṁ payojeti—
root_text.an5.50:1.5: Atha kho muṇḍo rājā piyakaṁ kosārakkhaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an5.50:1.6: “tena hi, samma piyaka, bhaddāya deviyā sarīraṁ āyasāya teladoṇiyā pakkhipitvā aññissā āyasāya doṇiyā paṭikujjatha, yathā mayaṁ bhaddāya deviyā sarīraṁ cirataraṁ passeyyāmā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:1.7: “Evaṁ, devā”ti kho piyako kosārakkho muṇḍassa rañño paṭissutvā bhaddāya deviyā sarīraṁ āyasāya teladoṇiyā pakkhipitvā aññissā āyasāya doṇiyā paṭikujji.
root_text.an5.50:2.2: “imassa kho muṇḍassa rañño bhaddā devī kālaṅkatā piyā manāpā.
root_text.an5.50:2.3: So bhaddāya deviyā kālaṅkatāya piyāya manāpāya neva nhāyati na vilimpati na bhattaṁ bhuñjati na kammantaṁ payojeti—
root_text.an5.50:2.5: Kaṁ nu kho muṇḍo rājā samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā payirupāseyya, yassa dhammaṁ sutvā sokasallaṁ pajaheyyā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:3.2: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā nārado pāṭaliputte viharati kukkuṭārāme.
root_text.an5.50:3.3: Taṁ kho panāyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
root_text.an5.50:3.4: ‘paṇḍito viyatto medhāvī bahussuto cittakathī kalyāṇapaṭibhāno vuddho ceva arahā ca’.
root_text.an5.50:3.5: Yannūna muṇḍo rājā āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ payirupāseyya, appeva nāma muṇḍo rājā āyasmato nāradassa dhammaṁ sutvā sokasallaṁ pajaheyyā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:4.1: Atha kho piyako kosārakkho yena muṇḍo rājā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā muṇḍaṁ rājānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:4.2: “ayaṁ kho, deva, āyasmā nārado pāṭaliputte viharati kukkuṭārāme.
root_text.an5.50:4.3: Taṁ kho panāyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
root_text.an5.50:4.4: ‘paṇḍito viyatto medhāvī bahussuto cittakathī kalyāṇapaṭibhāno vuddho ceva arahā ca’.
root_text.an5.50:4.5: Yadi pana devo āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ payirupāseyya, appeva nāma devo āyasmato nāradassa dhammaṁ sutvā sokasallaṁ pajaheyyā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:4.6: “Tena hi, samma piyaka, āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ paṭivedehi.
root_text.an5.50:4.7: Kathañhi nāma mādiso samaṇaṁ vā brāhmaṇaṁ vā vijite vasantaṁ pubbe appaṭisaṁvidito upasaṅkamitabbaṁ maññeyyā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:4.8: “Evaṁ, devā”ti kho piyako kosārakkho muṇḍassa rañño paṭissutvā yenāyasmā nārado tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho piyako kosārakkho āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:5.1: “Imassa, bhante, muṇḍassa rañño bhaddā devī kālaṅkatā piyā manāpā.
root_text.an5.50:5.2: So bhaddāya deviyā kālaṅkatāya piyāya manāpāya neva nhāyati na vilimpati na bhattaṁ bhuñjati na kammantaṁ payojeti—
root_text.an5.50:5.4: Sādhu, bhante, āyasmā nārado muṇḍassa rañño tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathā muṇḍo rājā āyasmato nāradassa dhammaṁ sutvā sokasallaṁ pajaheyyā”ti.
root_text.an5.50:6.1: Atha kho piyako kosārakkho uṭṭhāyāsanā āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena muṇḍo rājā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā muṇḍaṁ rājānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:6.4: “Tena hi, samma piyaka, bhadrāni bhadrāni yānāni yojāpehī”ti.
root_text.an5.50:6.5: “Evaṁ, devā”ti kho piyako kosārakkho muṇḍassa rañño paṭissutvā bhadrāni bhadrāni yānāni yojāpetvā muṇḍaṁ rājānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:6.6: “yuttāni kho te, deva, bhadrāni bhadrāni yānāni.
root_text.an5.50:7.1: Atha kho muṇḍo rājā bhadraṁ yānaṁ abhiruhitvā bhadrehi bhadrehi yānehi yena kukkuṭārāmo tena pāyāsi mahaccā rājānubhāvena āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an5.50:7.2: Yāvatikā yānassa bhūmi yānena gantvā, yānā paccorohitvā pattikova ārāmaṁ pāvisi.
root_text.an5.50:7.3: Atha kho muṇḍo rājā yena āyasmā nārado tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho muṇḍaṁ rājānaṁ āyasmā nārado etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:8.1: “Pañcimāni, mahārāja, alabbhanīyāni ṭhānāni samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.50:8.3: ‘Jarādhammaṁ mā jīrī’ti alabbhanīyaṁ ṭhānaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.50:8.4: ‘Byādhidhammaṁ mā byādhīyī’ti …pe… ‘maraṇadhammaṁ mā mīyī’ti … ‘khayadhammaṁ mā khīyī’ti … ‘nassanadhammaṁ mā nassī’ti alabbhanīyaṁ ṭhānaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.50:9.2: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe na iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an5.50:9.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.50:9.4: Ahañceva kho pana jarādhamme jiṇṇe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.50:9.5: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.50:9.7: ‘assutavā puthujjano viddho savisena sokasallena attānaṁyeva paritāpeti’.
root_text.an5.50:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, mahārāja, assutavato puthujjanassa byādhidhammaṁ byādhīyati …pe… maraṇadhammaṁ mīyati … khayadhammaṁ khīyati … nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.50:10.2: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe na iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an5.50:10.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa nassanadhammaṁ nassati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.50:10.4: Ahañceva kho pana nassanadhamme naṭṭhe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.50:10.5: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.50:10.7: ‘assutavā puthujjano viddho savisena sokasallena attānaṁyeva paritāpeti’.
root_text.an5.50:11.1: Sutavato ca kho, mahārāja, ariyasāvakassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.50:11.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.50:11.4: Ahañceva kho pana jarādhamme jiṇṇe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.50:11.5: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.50:11.7: ‘sutavā ariyasāvako abbuhi savisaṁ sokasallaṁ, yena viddho assutavā puthujjano attānaṁyeva paritāpeti.
root_text.an5.50:12.1: Puna caparaṁ, mahārāja, sutavato ariyasāvakassa byādhidhammaṁ byādhīyati …pe… maraṇadhammaṁ mīyati … khayadhammaṁ khīyati … nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.50:12.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa nassanadhammaṁ nassati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.50:12.4: Ahañceva kho pana nassanadhamme naṭṭhe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.50:12.5: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.50:12.7: ‘sutavā ariyasāvako abbuhi savisaṁ sokasallaṁ, yena viddho assutavā puthujjano attānaṁyeva paritāpeti.
root_text.an5.50:13.1: Imāni kho, mahārāja, pañca alabbhanīyāni ṭhānāni samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasminti.
root_text.an5.50:18.1: Evaṁ vutte, muṇḍo rājā āyasmantaṁ nāradaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.50:18.5: Imañhi me, bhante, dhammapariyāyaṁ sutvā sokasallaṁ pahīnan”ti.
root_text.an5.50:19.1: Atha kho muṇḍo rājā piyakaṁ kosārakkhaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an5.50:19.2: “tena hi, samma piyaka, bhaddāya deviyā sarīraṁ jhāpetha; thūpañcassā karotha.
root_text.an5.50:19.3: Ajjatagge dāni mayaṁ nhāyissāma ceva vilimpissāma bhattañca bhuñjissāma kammante ca payojessāmā”ti.

translation_text.an5.50:1.1: At one time Venerable Nārada was staying near Pāṭaliputta, in the Chicken Monastery.
translation_text.an5.50:1.2: Now at that time King Muṇḍa’s dear and beloved Queen Bhaddā had just passed away.
translation_text.an5.50:1.3: And since that time, the king did not bathe, anoint himself, eat his meals, or apply himself to his work.
translation_text.an5.50:1.4: Day and night he brooded over Queen Bhaddā’s corpse.
translation_text.an5.50:1.5: Then King Muṇḍa addressed Piyaka, the keeper of the treasury,
translation_text.an5.50:1.6: “So, my good Piyaka, please place Queen Bhaddā’s corpse in an iron casket filled with oil. Then close it up with another casket, so that we can view Queen Bhaddā’s body even longer.”
translation_text.an5.50:1.7: “Yes, Your Majesty,” replied Piyaka, and he did as the king instructed.
translation_text.an5.50:2.2: “King Muṇḍa’s dear and beloved Queen Bhaddā has passed away.
translation_text.an5.50:2.3: Since then the king does not bathe, anoint himself, eat his meals, or apply himself to his work.
translation_text.an5.50:2.4: Day and night he broods over Queen Bhaddā’s corpse.
translation_text.an5.50:2.5: Now, what ascetic or brahmin might the king pay homage to, whose teaching could help the king give up sorrow’s arrow?”
translation_text.an5.50:3.2: “This Venerable Nārada is staying in the Chicken Monastery at Pāṭaliputta.
translation_text.an5.50:3.4: ‘He is astute, competent, intelligent, learned, a brilliant speaker, eloquent, mature, a perfected one.’
translation_text.an5.50:3.5: What if King Muṇḍa was to pay homage to Venerable Nārada? Hopefully when he hears Nārada’s teaching, the king could give up sorrow’s arrow.”
translation_text.an5.50:4.1: Then Piyaka went to the king and said to him,
translation_text.an5.50:4.2: “Sire, this Venerable Nārada is staying in the Chicken Monastery at Pāṭaliputta.
translation_text.an5.50:4.4: ‘He is astute, competent, intelligent, learned, a brilliant speaker, eloquent, mature, a perfected one.’
translation_text.an5.50:4.5: What if Your Majesty was to pay homage to Venerable Nārada? Hopefully when you hear Nārada’s teaching, you could give up sorrow’s arrow.”
translation_text.an5.50:4.6: “Well then, my good Piyaka, let Nārada know.
translation_text.an5.50:4.7: For how on earth could one such as I presume to visit an ascetic or brahmin in my realm without letting them know beforehand?”
translation_text.an5.50:4.8: “Yes, Your Majesty,” replied Piyaka. He went to Nārada, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an5.50:5.1: “Sir, King Muṇḍa’s dear and beloved Queen Bhaddā has passed away.
translation_text.an5.50:5.2: And since she passed away, the king has not bathed, anointed himself, eaten his meals, or got his business done.
translation_text.an5.50:5.3: Day and night he broods over Queen Bhaddā’s corpse.
translation_text.an5.50:5.4: Sir, please teach the king so that, when he hears your teaching, he can give up sorrow’s arrow.”
translation_text.an5.50:5.5: “Please, Piyaka, let the king come when he likes.”
translation_text.an5.50:6.1: Then Piyaka got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled Venerable Nārada, keeping him on his right, before going to the king and saying,
translation_text.an5.50:6.2: “Sire, the request for an audience with Venerable Nārada has been granted.
translation_text.an5.50:6.3: Please, Your Majesty, go at your convenience.”
translation_text.an5.50:6.4: “Well then, my good Piyaka, harness the finest chariots.”
translation_text.an5.50:6.5: “Yes, Your Majesty,” replied Piyaka. He did so, then told the king:
translation_text.an5.50:6.7: Please, Your Majesty, go at your convenience.”
translation_text.an5.50:7.1: Then King Muṇḍa mounted a fine carriage and, along with other fine carriages, set out in full royal pomp to see Venerable Nārada at the Chicken Monastery.
translation_text.an5.50:7.2: He went by carriage as far as the terrain allowed, then descended and entered the monastery on foot.
translation_text.an5.50:7.3: Then the king went up to Nārada, bowed, and sat down to one side. Then Nārada said to him:
translation_text.an5.50:8.1: “Great king, there are five things that cannot be had by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.50:8.3: That someone liable to old age should not grow old. …
translation_text.an5.50:8.4: That someone liable to sickness should not get sick. … That someone liable to death should not die. … That someone liable to ending should not end. … That someone liable to perishing should not perish. …
translation_text.an5.50:9.1: An unlearned ordinary person has someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.50:9.2: But they don’t reflect on old age:
translation_text.an5.50:9.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to old age who grows old. For all sentient beings have someone liable to old age who grows old, as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn.
translation_text.an5.50:9.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to old age grows old, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.50:9.5: And so, when someone liable to old age grows old, they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.50:9.7: an unlearned ordinary person struck by sorrow’s poisoned arrow, who only mortifies themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:10.1: Furthermore, an unlearned ordinary person has someone liable to sickness … death … ending … perishing.
translation_text.an5.50:10.2: But they don’t reflect on perishing:
translation_text.an5.50:10.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to perishing who perishes. For all sentient beings have someone liable to perishing who perishes, as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn.
translation_text.an5.50:10.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to perishing perishes, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.50:10.5: And so, when someone liable to perishing perishes, they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.50:10.7: an unlearned ordinary person struck by sorrow’s poisoned arrow, who only mortifies themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:11.1: A learned noble disciple has someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.50:11.2: So they reflect on old age:
translation_text.an5.50:11.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to old age who grows old. For all sentient beings have someone liable to old age who grows old, as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn.
translation_text.an5.50:11.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to old age grows old, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.50:11.5: And so, when someone liable to old age grows old, they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.50:11.7: a learned noble disciple who has drawn out sorrow’s poisoned arrow, struck by which unlearned ordinary people only mortify themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:11.8: Sorrowless, free of thorns, that noble disciple only extinguishes themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:12.1: Furthermore, a learned noble disciple has someone liable to sickness … death … ending … perishing.
translation_text.an5.50:12.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to perishing who perishes. For all sentient beings have someone liable to perishing who perishes, as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn.
translation_text.an5.50:12.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to perishing perishes, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.50:12.5: And so, when someone liable to perishing perishes, they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.50:12.7: a learned noble disciple who has drawn out sorrow’s poisoned arrow, struck by which unlearned ordinary people only mortify themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:12.8: Sorrowless, free of thorns, that noble disciple only extinguishes themselves.
translation_text.an5.50:13.1: These are the five things that cannot be had by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.50:14.2: doesn’t do even a little bit of good.
translation_text.an5.50:14.3: When they know that you’re sad,
translation_text.an5.50:15.1: When an astute person doesn’t waver <j>in the face of adversity,
translation_text.an5.50:15.2: as they’re able to evaluate what’s beneficial,
translation_text.an5.50:15.4: seeing that their normal expression doesn’t change.
translation_text.an5.50:16.3: if by means of any such things you benefit,
translation_text.an5.50:16.4: then by all means keep doing them.
translation_text.an5.50:17.1: But if you understand that ‘this good thing
translation_text.an5.50:17.2: can’t be had by me or by anyone else’,
translation_text.an5.50:17.3: you should accept it without sorrowing, thinking:
translation_text.an5.50:17.4: ‘The deed is powerful. What can I do now?’”
translation_text.an5.50:18.1: When he said this, King Muṇḍa said to Venerable Nārada,
translation_text.an5.50:18.2: “Sir, what is the name of this exposition of the teaching?”
translation_text.an5.50:18.3: “Great king, this exposition of the teaching is named ‘Pulling Out Sorrow’s Arrow’.”
translation_text.an5.50:18.4: “Indeed, sir, this is the pulling out of sorrow’s arrow!
translation_text.an5.50:18.5: Hearing this exposition of the teaching, I’ve given up sorrow’s arrow.”
translation_text.an5.50:19.1: Then King Muṇḍa addressed Piyaka, the keeper of the treasury,
translation_text.an5.50:19.2: “Well then, my good Piyaka, cremate Queen Bhaddā’s corpse and build a monument.
translation_text.an5.50:19.3: From this day forth, I will bathe, anoint myself, eat my meals, and apply myself to my work.”

variant_text.an5.50:3.4: vuddho ceva → vuḍḍho ceva (sya-all); buddho ceva (mr) | arahā ca → arahā cā’ti (?)
variant_text.an5.50:7.1: bhadraṁ yānaṁ → bhadrāni bhadrāni yānāni (sya-all, km); bhaddaṁ yānaṁ (pts1ed); bhadraṁ bhadraṁ yānaṁ (mr) | mahaccā → mahacca (bahūsu)
